pages.text: The twelve essence metals belong to four elemental pillars. Their restraint and symbiosis form the core logic of Chaos Alchemy and govern every later reaction.

pages.title: Astral Flame
pages.text: Eclipsium, Lunargent, and Astrotite. Astral Flame restrains Calamitous Wind and is restrained by Abyssal Water.

pages.title: Abyssal Water
pages.text: Abyssion, Animercury, and Necroplumb. Abyssal Water restrains Astral Flame and is restrained by Blighted Earth.

pages.title: Blighted Earth
pages.text: Sanguibronze, Venotite, and Ossantimony. Blighted Earth restrains Abyssal Water and is restrained by Calamitous Wind.

pages.title: Calamitous Wind
pages.text: Fulgurzinc, Chronoplatinum, and Pandemonium. Calamitous Wind restrains Blighted Earth and is restrained by Astral Flame.

pages.title: Relations
pages.text: The pillar cycle is:$(br2)$(term)Astral Flame → Calamitous Wind → Blighted Earth → Abyssal Water → Astral Flame$().$(br2)The restraining essence activates while the restrained essence deteriorates. Metal-specific relations may add a second restraint, create mutual restraint, or link two essences in symbiosis so both activate.

pages.title: Pandemonium
pages.text: Pandemonium is the great exception: it restrains all eleven other essences and is restrained by none. Its rarity matches its value in high alchemy.

pages.title: Emerald Tablet
pages.text: Crafted from one Transmutation Crystal and four emeralds. Right-click while holding it to inspect the complete relationship between any two essences.

pages.title: Four States
pages.text: Each essence has four states: Nigredo (-1), base (0), Albedo (+1), and Citrinitas (+2). A state changes its degree, never the essence's identity or relationships.

pages.title: Beyond the Bounds
pages.text: Below Nigredo, an essence collapses into alchemical dross. Above Citrinitas, it burns out into redstone dust. Neither remnant retains alchemical use.
